senses.definition: the collective name for the radical urban working-class political group in revolutionary France known as the sans-culottes
examples: The pamphlet praised the sansculotteries for their zeal in the revolution.
Students studied the role of the sansculotteries in shaping fiscal policy during the early Republic.

senses.definition: a member of the common people in revolutionary France who wore long trousers instead of breeches and supported the more radical elements of the revolution
examples: The sansculottes demanded price controls and direct democracy.
During the revolution, the sansculottes were influential in protests.

senses.definition: relating to the sans-culottes or their beliefs, often describing radical or populist elements of the French Revolution
examples: A sansculottic stance favored direct democracy and price controls.
They discussed sansculottic ideology in their political manifestos.

senses.definition: a unit of time in ancient France used to measure a day in the French Republican Calendar (related etymologically to sans-culottes); often used in historical or scholarly contexts
examples: The token 'sansculottid' appears in the calendar tables of revolutionary-era manuscripts.
Scholars discussed sansculottids in their analysis of calendar reform.

senses.definition: the five or more days at the end of the French Republican calendar month, associated with parliamentary and festival activities
examples: The sansculottides marked the end of the month in the Republican calendar.
Scholars sometimes note the sansculottides when studying revolutionary timekeeping.

senses.definition: relating to the sans-culottes or their characteristics; marked by radical or populist sentiments
examples: The pamphlet described a sansculottish mood among the crowd.
Some scholars used sansculottish rhetoric to describe revolutionary activism.

senses.definition: the political ideology or movement associated with the sans-culottes; radical populism in the French Revolution
examples: Sansculottism influenced revolutionary policy debates.
They debated the limits of sansculottism in governance.

senses.definition: plural form referring to various beliefs, practices, or expressions associated with sansculottism
examples: The treatise catalogues several sansculottisms observed during the era.
Historians compare different sansculottisms across regions.

senses.definition: a person who supports or belongs to the sans-culottes or sansculottism
examples: The pamphlet quotes a prominent sansculottist.
Many sansculottists participated in local assemblies.

senses.definition: plural of sansculottist; multiple supporters of sans-culottism
examples: The meeting was attended by several sansculottists.
Sansculottists debated policy in the assembly.

senses.definition: an eldest son who takes over family responsibilities in certain East Asian contexts; also a term in Japanese culture referring to a male who has returned from abroad
examples: In some families, the sansei helps care for elderly relatives.
The sansei returned with a broadened view of the world.

senses.definition: plural of sansei; multiple eldest sons or returning young men
examples: The sanseis formed a social club.
Families with sanseis often maintain strong traditions.

senses.definition: a typeface that has plain, straight-edged letters without serifs; also called grotesque or grotesk in some contexts
examples: The logo uses a clean sanserif font for modern look.
Most web body text appears in a sans-serif font.

senses.definition: plural of sanserif; multiple sans-serif fonts or typefaces
examples: The designer compared several sanserifs before choosing one.
Sanserifs are popular for digital screens.

senses.definition: a genus of flowering plants in the family Asparagaceae, commonly known as snake plants; hardy houseplants
examples: She grows a sansevieria on her sunny windowsill.
Sansevierias are popular houseplants because they tolerate neglect.

senses.definition: plural of sansevieria; multiple snake plants
examples: Her kitchen featured several sansevierias.
Sansevierias are drought-tolerant houseplants.

senses.definition: a person who speaks a language in a way that is socially inappropriate or nonstandard (often slang or pejorative); historically used in some contexts to describe a poor speaker of a language
examples: The guide scolded the tourist for sounding like a sanshach.
The term is considered offensive in some communities.

senses.definition: a person who speaks a particular language in a ceremonial or formal context; also an informal self-designation for a group in some communities
examples: The elder spoke as a sani guide to visitors (note: example for instructional context).
In the story, a sani explains local customs.

senses.definition: an ancient Indo-Aryan language of India, the classical language of Hindu and Buddhist texts
examples: Sanskrit is studied in traditional schools and universities.
Many ancient texts were originally written in Sanskrit.

senses.definition: relating to Sanskrit language or its grammar; pure or heavily influenced by Sanskrit in style
examples: The text uses a sanskritic vocabulary.
Her prose has a sanskritic cadence.

senses.definition: a person who studies Sanskrit, especially a scholar of Sanskrit literature or linguistics
examples: The Sanskritist lectured on Vedanta texts.
Sanskritists often translate ancient scriptures.

senses.definition: the process of incorporating or adopting Sanskrit elements into language, culture, or social practices; often used in sociolinguistic or historical contexts
examples: Sanskritization influenced naming conventions in some communities.
Scholars discuss Sanskritization as a historical linguistic trend.

senses.definition: to make something Sanskrit in form or style; to adopt Sanskrit elements into language or culture
examples: Some scholars attempted to Sanskritize technical vocabulary.
Communities Sanskritize daily rituals to align with traditional norms.

senses.definition: a term used in certain languages/cultures to denote a saint or holy person; also used as a respectful title in some contexts
examples: The sant was revered for his mercy.
Pilgrims visited the sant’s shrine.

senses.definition: a female saint or a woman named Santa; in popular culture, often used to refer to Santa Claus in some languages
examples: Children left cookies for Santa.
The Santa statue stood in the town square.
synonyms: referring to Santa Claus in some languages
